About Urban Climate Tools

Urban Climate Tools is a comprehensive, open-source database of solutions for building climate-resilient cities. We connect municipal leaders, planners, and communities with evidence-based tools for adaptation and mitigation.

Our Mission

To make climate adaptation accessible and actionable by curating and showcasing the world's best tools for building resilient urban communities.

We believe that cities need practical, proven solutions to address the growing impacts of climate change. By bringing together tools for heat adaptation, flood resilience, coastal protection, green infrastructure, and more, we help cities make informed decisions about their climate future.

What We Offer

Curated Catalog

Hand-selected tools from leading organizations, filtered by cost, skill level, and geographic scope.

Searchable Database

Find the right tool for your city with advanced filtering and full-text search across our catalog.

Case Studies

Learn from real-world implementations in cities around the world adapting to climate impacts.

Open Source

All content is freely available and contributions are welcome from the global community.

Categories

Heat Management

Urban heat island mitigation, cool roofs, green spaces, and heat warning systems.

Flood Risk

Stormwater management, permeable surfaces, green infrastructure for flood resilience.

Coastal Resilience

Sea level rise adaptation, coastal protection, and community planning tools.

Green Infrastructure

Nature-based solutions, urban forestry, biodiversity, and ecosystem services.

Urban Planning

Resilience planning, land use tools, and sustainable development frameworks.

Data & Analytics

Climate data platforms, modeling tools, and visualization dashboards.

Energy Efficiency

Energy modeling, consumption tracking, and emissions monitoring.

Other Tools

Additional resources and utilities for climate adaptation and sustainability.

How to Use This Site

  1. Browse or Search

    Use our search bar or filter by category, cost, skill level, and geographic coverage.

  2. Read Descriptions

    Each tool page includes detailed descriptions, features, and use cases.

  3. Check Resources

    Find links to documentation, GitHub repositories, and support information.

  4. Learn from Case Studies

    Explore real-world implementations in cities addressing climate challenges.

  5. Share and Contribute

    Share tools with your network and contribute new tools or case studies.

Contributing

We welcome contributions from the community! You can:

  • Suggest new tools to add to the database
  • Share case studies from your city or organization
  • Improve documentation and descriptions
  • Report issues or broken links
  • Contribute code improvements to the site

Contribute on GitHub

About the Project

Urban Climate Tools is an open-source project created by the Urban Climate Adaptation Network. We maintain and curate this database in collaboration with:

  • Municipal leaders and urban planners
  • Climate science researchers
  • Environmental organizations
  • Technology developers
  • Community organizations

Frequently Asked Questions

How often is the database updated?

We update the database regularly as new tools are developed and existing tools evolve. Major updates occur quarterly with continuous community contributions.

Can I suggest a tool?

Absolutely! Please submit a GitHub issue or pull request with information about the tool you'd like to add.

Are all tools free?

No, we include tools across the pricing spectrum. Filter by "Free" or "Freemium" to see low-cost options. We believe in showcasing the full range of available solutions.

What if I find a broken link or outdated information?

Please report it via GitHub Issues so we can update it. We appreciate community help in keeping the database current.

Can I use this data for my project?

Yes! All content is available under an open license. Please attribute Urban Climate Tools and link back to the original source.

How can my organization get involved?

We welcome partnerships and collaborations. Contact us at info@urbanclimate.tools to discuss opportunities.

Get in Touch

Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you!
